name: @dotta
description: Creator of Paperclip, the AI coding agent runtime. Posts from direct build experience, not commentary. Detailed on failure modes and system design.
reviewNote: Original work, specific, testable. One of the few practitioners posting real architectural decisions with real trade-offs.

name: @shipper_now
description: Documents the process of shipping products fast with agentic workflows. Operational specifics, not theory.
reviewNote: Consistent original output about build velocity and agentic tooling. Shows the work.

name: @swyx
description: AI engineer and writer. Runs latent.space. Tracks the infrastructure and capability curve of LLM tooling with engineer-level rigor.
reviewNote: Deep research, well-sourced, transparent about uncertainty. One of the best for understanding where the stack is heading.

name: @karpathy
description: First principles explanations of how LLMs and agents actually work. Posts from direct research and implementation experience, including foundational work at OpenAI and Tesla.
reviewNote: Foundational. Anyone building with LLMs should have a working understanding of what Karpathy publishes.

name: @mckaywrigley
description: Builder of open-source AI interfaces and coding tools. Created Chatbot UI, one of the earliest widely-used open-source LLM frontends. Posts on AI engineering patterns and developer tooling.
reviewNote: Prolific builder, shows code. Representative of the practitioner-first AI builder community.

name: @amasad
description: Replit CEO. Building AI-native development environments where agents write, run, and deploy code in the same loop. Posts on the future of software creation.
reviewNote: Building the infrastructure layer where AI writes and executes code. High signal on agentic dev environments.

name: @jxnlco
description: Creator of the Instructor library for structured LLM outputs. Posts practical patterns for reliable AI output validation, function calling, and production-ready LLM systems.
reviewNote: One of the most actionable voices on making LLMs actually reliable in production. Instructor is in active use across the agentic stack ecosystem.

name: @FelixCraftAI
description: Building AI-native products and documenting what actually works. Posts operational signal on agentic workflows and product development with AI.
reviewNote: Added by Shant — direct build experience in agentic product space.

name: @Nateliason
description: Writer, builder, and entrepreneur. Posts on using AI to run lean operations and build with small teams. Specific about what tools and systems he actually ships with.
reviewNote: Added by Shant — known for practical, operator-level AI usage, not hype.

name: @sukh_saroy
description: Building with AI and documenting what works in practice. Posts operational signal on agentic workflows and AI-native product development.

name: @johannfyi
description: Ex-Cisco security engineer. Publishes practical security guides for OpenClaw deployments and agentic infrastructure. First-hand practitioner, not a commentator.
reviewNote: Author of OpenClaw Security 101 — the guide we used to harden our own VPS.

name: @MatthewBerman
description: AI researcher and builder. Posts hands-on evaluations of frontier models, open-source releases, and agentic tools. One of the most consistent sources of first-look coverage on what actually ships.
reviewNote: Added by Shant. High signal on model releases and open-source AI tooling.
